stories.headline: CEOs Boast of Agentic AI Code Output as Industry Shifts
stories.summary: Top executives, including Meta's Mark Zuckerberg, are increasingly highlighting the volume of AI-generated code shipped by their companies. This trend signals a broader industry shift towards agentic AI transforming software engineering workflows beyond just Big Tech.
stories.original_title: [Business Insider] The new CEO flex: Bragging about how much AI code your company shipped — CEOs like Meta's Mark Zuckerberg can't stop talking about how agentic AI is changing how engineers code — and it's not just the Big Tech bosses.
stories.context: For decades, writing computer code was a highly specialized skill that required years of training, much like learning to speak a foreign language fluently. Recently, artificial intelligence tools have improved to the point where they can understand human instructions and write software programs automatically, acting like a tireless junior assistant. This shift is significant because it allows companies to build digital products much faster and with fewer human programmers, fundamentally changing how the technology industry operates. Executives are now boasting about this because it proves that AI is no longer just a novelty but a core part of how modern businesses create value.
stories.why_it_matters: AI is now writing computer code faster than humans, which means software gets built quicker and cheaper. This changes how tech companies work and could lead to more apps and tools being created by fewer people.
stories.other_side: Critics worry that relying on AI for code might introduce hidden errors or security holes that are hard to fix. There is also fear that human programmers could lose their jobs as machines take over routine coding tasks.

stories.headline: Memory Stocks Surge on AI-Driven Demand
stories.summary: The tech sector is seeing a rally in memory stocks as artificial intelligence drives unprecedented demand for hardware. Companies like SanDisk, Micron, and Samsung are leading this charge as investors bet on the infrastructure needs of AI.
stories.original_title: [Business Insider] Memory stocks are the tech sector's hottest trade. Here's why. — Memory stocks are soaring as AI drives demand. Sandisk, Micron, and Sansung are some of the stocks driving the rally.
stories.context: Modern computers and smartphones rely on special chips called memory storage to hold the data they are actively using, similar to how your short-term memory holds information for a few seconds. Artificial intelligence requires massive amounts of this fast memory to process complex tasks like recognizing images or understanding language in real time. Because AI is growing so rapidly, companies need to buy huge quantities of these memory chips, causing their prices and stock values to rise sharply. Investors are betting on this trend because every new AI application needs more of this physical hardware to function.
stories.why_it_matters: AI needs huge amounts of memory to think, so companies making memory chips are making a lot of money. This shows that the AI boom is driving real physical demand for hardware, not just software ideas.
stories.other_side: Some experts warn that this rush to build hardware might lead to a bubble where prices crash if AI growth slows down. Others point out that the energy needed to run these chips is bad for the environment.

stories.headline: Tom Lee Predicts $200,000 Bitcoin as Crypto Defends Support
stories.summary: Crypto analyst Tom Lee has set a year-end target of $200,000 for Bitcoin, signaling the end of the crypto winter as the asset defends crucial support levels. The outlook also includes massive projections for Ethereum amidst broader market recovery signs.
stories.original_title: [newsBTC] Tom Lee Sets $200,000 Year-End Bitcoin Target As Price Defends Crucial Support — As Bitcoin (BTC) defends a pivotal support level, Tom Lee has called for the end of the crypto winter, setting massive year-end outlooks for the flagship crypto and Ethereum (ETH). Related Reading: DeFi Platform TrustedVolumes Hit By $6.7M Hack As 2026 Exploi…
stories.context: Bitcoin is a type of digital money that exists only on the internet, designed to work without banks or governments controlling it, which makes its price very volatile and unpredictable. When people say an asset "defends support," they mean that buyers are stepping in at a certain low price point to prevent the value from dropping further, showing strong confidence in the currency. Analysts like Tom Lee predict massive future prices based on the idea that more people and institutions will adopt Bitcoin as a safe place to store wealth, similar to gold. This optimism suggests that the recent period of falling prices, known as "crypto winter," is ending as interest returns to the market.
stories.why_it_matters: If Bitcoin hits $200,000, it proves that digital money can become a major part of the global economy. This would encourage more people and businesses to trust and use cryptocurrencies for everyday transactions.
stories.other_side: Many financial experts say Bitcoin is too risky and volatile, comparing it to gambling rather than investing. They warn that such high prices are unlikely and could lead to massive losses for regular people who buy in late.

stories.headline: Buggy Fitbit Update Blinds Pixel Watch Sensors
stories.summary: A recent buggy update from Fitbit has caused sensor failures on the Pixel Watch 3 and 4, undermining trust in Google's health tracking ecosystem. This technical glitch threatens Google's strategy to expand its wearable health services through the Fitbit Air initiative.
stories.original_title: [Android Central] A buggy Fitbit update is blinding sensors on the Pixel Watch 3 and 4 — Google’s new Fitbit Air aims to expand its health ecosystem, but a buggy update is hurting trust in Pixel Watch health tracking.
stories.context: Smartwatches like the Pixel Watch use small sensors on the back of the device to measure things like your heart rate and sleep patterns by shining light into your skin. Sometimes, when companies send out automatic software updates to fix bugs or add features, these updates can accidentally break the communication between the software and these delicate sensors. In this case, a faulty update from Fitbit caused the watches to stop working correctly, leaving users unable to track their health data accurately. This is frustrating for customers because it undermines their trust in the device's ability to monitor their well-being reliably.
stories.why_it_matters: A simple software glitch breaking health sensors shows how fragile our connected devices really are. It reminds users that their personal health data depends on reliable technology that can fail without warning.
stories.other_side: Google might argue that bugs happen during updates and that they fix them quickly, so it’s not a big deal. Some users may not care about one glitch if they like the rest of the device's features.

stories.headline: No-Code App Built to Protect Elderly from Scams
stories.summary: A developer created a no-code application called ScamSkeptic using Cursor to protect his aging family from digital fraud. This story highlights the growing accessibility of AI-assisted coding tools for solving specific, personal security challenges.
stories.original_title: [Business Insider] My aging family kept falling for scams. For $20, I built a no-code app to protect them. — Matter Sanner, 54, vibe coded an app using Cursor called ScamSkeptic for his aging family, who had fallen victim to scams.
stories.context: Traditionally, building a computer program required learning complex coding languages that took years to master, excluding most people from creating their own digital tools. "No-code" platforms have changed this by allowing users to build apps using simple drag-and-drop interfaces or natural language prompts instead of typing code. A developer recently used an AI tool called Cursor to quickly create an app that helps older relatives identify and avoid online scams, solving a specific family problem without needing deep technical skills. This highlights a major trend where AI lowers the barrier to entry, enabling everyday people to solve personal security issues through custom software.
stories.why_it_matters: This story proves you don’t need to be a computer expert to build useful tools with AI help. It empowers everyday people to solve their own problems, like protecting family from scams, using simple drag-and-drop tools.
stories.other_side: Relying on no-code tools might mean the resulting app isn’t very secure or stable compared to professionally coded software. There is also a risk that non-experts might create tools that accidentally violate privacy laws.

stories.headline: Hogwarts Legacy Remains Dominant Ahead of Sequel
stories.summary: Hogwarts Legacy continues to perform strongly across multiple storefronts as it approaches its third anniversary. The game's sustained popularity keeps fans engaged while anticipation builds for the upcoming sequel in the franchise.
stories.original_title: [Screen Rant] Hogwarts Legacy: Wizard Academy Completely Changes The Game — Hogwarts Legacy is coming up on its third anniversary this year, and it's still going strong on multiple storefronts as fans wait for the sequel.
stories.context: Hogwarts Legacy is a video game set in the magical world of Harry Potter, where players explore a large open world and learn spells as students at a wizard school. Unlike many games that fade away after release, this title has remained incredibly popular for nearly three years because of its high-quality graphics, engaging story, and constant updates. Its sustained success is rare in the gaming industry, where most titles see a sharp drop in players within months. Fans are now eagerly waiting for a sequel, hoping the developers can capture the same magic while expanding on the original game's formula.
stories.why_it_matters: The game’s long-lasting success shows that players still crave deep, immersive experiences over quick trends. It proves that high-quality storytelling and world-building can keep fans engaged for years, benefiting the entire gaming industry.
stories.other_side: Some critics argue that the game relies heavily on nostalgia rather than innovative gameplay mechanics. They also worry that the sequel might struggle to live up to the original’s hype, leading to disappointment.

stories.headline: India's Mining Sector Eyes Tech-Driven Job Growth
stories.summary: A new report suggests India's mining sector could create 25 million jobs by 2047 through a structural transition driven by emerging technologies. The 'Mining 5.0' framework highlights how rising mineral demand will be met with advanced technological integration.
stories.original_title: [BusinessLine] India's mining sector can create 25 million jobs by 2047: Report — The report “Mining 5.0 - Emerging Mining Technologies by 2030” states that India’s mining industry is undergoing a “structural transition” due to rising mineral demand
stories.context: Mining involves digging up valuable minerals from the earth, a process that has traditionally been dangerous, dirty, and reliant on heavy manual labor. India is looking to transform this old-school industry by introducing "Mining 5.0," which means using advanced technologies like robots, drones, and artificial intelligence to make mining safer and more efficient. The goal is to meet the rising global demand for minerals needed for electronics and green energy while creating millions of new jobs in technology and engineering rather than just manual digging. This shift represents a move toward a smarter, cleaner industrial future that leverages innovation to solve resource challenges.
stories.why_it_matters: Using technology in mining could create millions of new jobs in India, boosting the local economy. It shows how modern tech can transform old industries into sources of employment and growth for developing nations.
stories.other_side: Environmental groups worry that increased mining activity will damage nature and pollute water sources. There is also concern that automation might replace low-skilled workers, leaving them without jobs.

stories.headline: Walmart Flash Deals Feature Significant Tech Discounts
stories.summary: Walmart has updated its Flash Deals section with significant discounts on top tech items, including cameras and TVs with up to 65% off. Shoppers are encouraged to act quickly before these limited-time offers sell out.
stories.original_title: [Dealnews.com] Walmart Flash Deals + free shipping w/ $35 — Walmart's Flash Deals section was just updated with new deals for the coming week, so now is the best time to shop before the best stuff sells out. Some discounts include up to 65% off top tech, like cameras and TVs, up to 40% off bikes, up to $100 off outdoo…
stories.context: Walmart's "Flash Deals" are limited-time sales events where popular items are offered at steep discounts for a short window, often lasting only a few hours or days. These deals are designed to create a sense of urgency, encouraging shoppers to buy immediately before the stock runs out or the offer expires. Recent promotions have featured significant price cuts on high-demand electronics like cameras and televisions, making premium tech more affordable for budget-conscious consumers. This strategy helps Walmart attract customers who might otherwise shop at specialized electronics stores, driving foot traffic and online visits during key shopping periods.
stories.why_it_matters: Big discounts on tech items make expensive gadgets affordable for more families. This helps spread technology into more homes, allowing more people to access cameras, TVs, and other digital tools.
stories.other_side: These limited-time deals can pressure shoppers into buying things they don’t actually need. Some consumers feel tricked by "flash" sales that aren’t as rare or cheap as they seem.

stories.headline: eBay Spring Promo Offers Extra 20% Off Tech and Electronics
stories.summary: eBay is offering an extra 20% off select items using the promo code SMARTWINS, covering a wide range of categories including tech and electronics. Free shipping is available on most orders, making it a notable deal for online shoppers.
stories.original_title: [Dealnews.com] eBay Spring Picks Promo Code + free shipping — Apply the promo code "SMARTWINS" to cut an extra 20% off everything in this eBay section. This includes tech, automotive gear, home and garden items, tools, and electronics. Shipping is free on most orders, too. The coupon can be used twice per account with a…
stories.context: eBay is an online marketplace where individuals and businesses can buy and sell goods directly to each other, often finding better deals than in traditional retail stores. To boost sales during the spring season, eBay introduced a promotional code called SMARTWINS that gives shoppers an extra 20% discount on select tech and electronic items. By combining these discounts with free shipping on most orders, eBay aims to remove common barriers to online shopping, such as high costs and delivery fees. This initiative encourages users to browse and purchase electronics they might have been hesitating to buy due to price concerns.
stories.why_it_matters: Extra discounts on electronics lower the cost of entry for students and budget-conscious buyers. This encourages more people to upgrade their devices or buy gifts, keeping the retail market active.
stories.other_side: Shoppers often complain that online promo codes are hard to find or don’t work at checkout. There is also frustration when popular items sell out instantly, leaving customers empty-handed.

stories.headline: STUF United Fund Proposes Tech Innovation for UN SDGs
stories.summary: The STUF United Fund participated in a UN forum focusing on science, technology, and innovation to support Sustainable Development Goal 17. The organization presented initiatives aimed at strengthening global partnerships through technological advancement.
stories.original_title: [Yahoo Entertainment] 世台聯合基金會參與聯合國論壇：聚焦SDG 17 提出科技創新倡議 — [Newtalk新聞] 世台聯合基金會（STUF United Fund）今（9）日表示，近日參與於聯合國總部舉行的第11屆聯合國永續發展目標科學、技術與創新多方利害關係人論壇（11th Multi-stakeholder Forum on Science, Technology and Innovation for the Sustainable Development Goals, STI Forum 2026），並聚焦「SDG 17：強化永續發展全球夥伴關係」（Partnerships for the Go…
stories.context: The United Nations has established Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs), which are a set of global targets aimed at solving major problems like poverty, inequality, and climate change by 2030. Goal 17 specifically focuses on strengthening global partnerships, meaning countries and organizations must work together more effectively to achieve these goals. The STUF United Fund participated in a UN forum to showcase how technology and innovation can facilitate better cooperation between different nations and sectors. Their presentation highlighted that modern tools and digital platforms can bridge gaps, making international collaboration faster, more transparent, and more impactful.
stories.why_it_matters: Using tech to support global goals like peace and partnership can solve big world problems faster. It shows how private funds and international organizations can team up to improve lives through innovation.
stories.other_side: Skeptics argue that technology alone cannot fix deep-rooted political or social issues. They worry that focusing on tech solutions might ignore the need for basic human rights and fair governance.
